methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Icarus is a seed-stage company in the Defense & Security sector founded by Henry Kwan. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of four same-sector, same- and adjacent-stage comparable companies: Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed). The broader comp universe includes two public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private peers spanning Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

Given the limited applicability of other valuation methods with available data, the peer-median approach provides the primary anchor. The four-company median valuation of $225M implies a centered estimate; the sample ranges from $60M to $500M, reflecting material dispersion across stage and maturity profiles. A triangulated range anchored on this single method yields $191M (low), $225M (median), and $259M (high), with the latter two bounding the interquartile range around the median.

Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of Series A comparables for a seed-stage asset—Stark Defence and Vatn Systems command substantial stage premiums—and the limited sample size. Tenna Systems at $60M represents the only pure seed comparable and may underweight Icarus if differentiation is material. Without additional revenue multiples, revenue traction, or DCF anchors, the $191M–$259M range should be treated as preliminary pending detailed due diligence on product maturity and customer traction.
